The company frames this heritage as transforming German coffee culture by making automated coffee preparation accessible while maintaining quality standards of manual filter brewing, reflecting kitchen technology evolution across successive design and lifestyle trends.

title: Melitta Group honored as Top Innovator Innovation Sustainability
title_en: Melitta Group Recognized as TOP 100 Innovator Among German Mid-Market Enterprises
summary_en: Melitta Group received the TOP 100 award recognizing exceptional innovation management through structured evaluation across five criteria: leadership, innovation climate, processes, external orientation, and success metrics. The award highlights digitalization and sustainability as key innovation drivers, citing examples including proprietary AI platform 'Melina', Avoury tea capsule technology, Toppits paper freezer bags, and 10X Innovation incubator in Berlin.
entities: Vienna University of Economics and Business

title: Learn more about sustainability at Julius Meinl
title_en: Julius Meinl Sustainability Framework: Origin, Planet, People Integration Across Value Chain
summary_en: Julius Meinl articulates a comprehensive sustainability strategy organized around three pillars: responsible coffee sourcing via Responsibly Selected Coffee Initiative and Generations Programme; carbon reduction through catalytic converter installations in roasters; and employee engagement as business resilience driver. The strategy reflects Julius Meinl's positioning as a fifth-generation family business with long-term accountability to supply-chain partners. ESG commitments include UN Global Compact membership and Global Coffee Platform governance participation.

title: 07.07.2026 Local talent takes centre stage at the Julius Meinl Barista Cup 2026
title_en: Julius Meinl Barista Cup 2026: International Talent Competition Across 15 Countries
summary_en: Julius Meinl held its annual Barista Cup across 15 countries, attracting over 350 applicants with approximately 100 advancing to local competitions. Competitors showcased skills in espresso, cappuccino, and signature drink categories. The 15 local champions competed in the Grand Finale in Vienna on 4 September 2026, with support from industry partners including Rancilio, Fiorenzato, BWT, and DaVinci Gourmet.

title: 20.06.2026 Explore the highlights of our 2025 Sustainability Report
title_en: Julius Meinl 2025 Sustainability Report: 100% Responsibly Sourced Coffee and Climate Resilience Expansion
summary_en: Julius Meinl achieved 100% 'Responsibly Selected Coffee' for globally procured green coffee by end of 2025, aligned with Global Coffee Platform baseline criteria. The company expanded its Generations Programme to four coffee-producing countries (Colombia, Uganda, India, Honduras) to build climate and financial resilience through regenerative farming. Additional achievements include catalytic converter installation on all roasting equipment (50% emissions reduction), 100% renewable electricity at Vienna and Vicenza production sites, 81% renewable electricity globally, and launch of a Human and Labour Rights Policy aligned with international standards.

title: Responsibly Selected Coffee Initiative
title_en: Julius Meinl Commits to 100% Responsibly Selected Coffee from 2026 via Global Coffee Platform Standards
summary_en: Julius Meinl announces that from 2026, all green coffee beans sourced for roasting at Vienna and Vicenza production plants will come from sustainability schemes recognized by the Global Coffee Platform (GCP) as aligned with the Coffee Sustainability Reference Code. The Code establishes baseline standards covering forest protection, child/forced labor prohibition, worker safety, and continuous improvement in soil health and biodiversity. Regular field assessments by Enveritas monitor compliance and identify improvements.

title: Collaborating for the Future of Coffee
title_en: Julius Meinl Supply Chain Strategy: Global Partnerships on Sustainability, Climate, and Farmer Resilience
summary_en: Julius Meinl's supply chain strategy integrates partnerships with Global Coffee Platform, UN Global Compact, coffee&climate initiative, and NGO Enveritas to address environmental and socioeconomic resilience. The company collaborates with suppliers, NGOs, farmer organizations, and government agencies in origin countries to support smallholder farmer adaptation to climate change. Select product lines carry Fairtrade and Rainforest Alliance certifications; coffee capsules achieve home-compostable certification (TÜV AUSTRIA).

title: #135 Lupine: Kaffeeinnovation und Superfood
title_en: Tchibo Develops Sweet Lupine-Coffee Blend to Address Climate Vulnerability
summary_en: Tchibo (Eduscho brand) launched "Kaffee und Lupine"—a coffee blend with regionally grown German sweet lupine designated 2026 Superfood. Product positions lupine as climate-resilient supplement rather than coffee substitute, addressing projected 50% reduction in global coffee cultivation area by 2050 while maintaining sensory quality (complex roast aromatics plus nutty lupine notes).

title: Der Name ist (Kaffee-)Programm: Peru
title_en: Tchibo Expands Coffee2Stay to Peru: Direct Farmer Partnership Amid Climate Pressure
summary_en: Tchibo integrated Peru (9th-largest global coffee producer, 3rd in South America) into Coffee2Stay program targeting Junín region where farmers face 50%+ harvest losses from pests, disease, and rainfall volatility. Peru supplies Tchibo brands (Latin Grande, Feine Milde, Beste Bohne, Barista). Program emphasizes direct farmer partnerships and climate-resilient methods over certification.

title: Tchibo Refurbished trifft Kundenbedarf
title_en: Tchibo Launches Refurbished Coffee Machine Program with Strong Market Response
summary_en: Tchibo introduced a refurbished coffee machine program in summer 2025, offering rebuilt machines with 24-month warranties through a take-back and resale model. The initiative prioritizes circular economy principles, leveraging Tchibo's 40+ years of designing repairable coffee systems with accessible components and stable spare-parts availability. Within months, the program showed strong customer demand for both purchasing refurbished machines and selling back used units, with Tchibo adjusting processes for profitability and viewing the model as scalable.

title: DAS Nachschlagewerk für Kaffee-Enthusiasten und Fans von Kaffee-Geschichten und Statistiken.
title_en: Tchibo Coffee Report 2026: EU Coffee Consumption Trends and Cross-Border Pricing Variance
summary_en: Tchibo's 15th annual coffee market report, based on 1,500 German coffee drinkers surveyed January 2026, reveals consumption patterns, price sensitivity, and consumer attitudes toward automation and sustainability. Cappuccino pricing varies significantly across EU markets—declining in Portugal, rising in Hungary—reflecting regional economic and inflation pressures. German consumers maintain regular daily consumption; post-16:00 coffee consumption drops sharply, and surveys show growing consumer skepticism about AI-driven coffee preparation.

title: Nah, näher, Farmkaffee: Kaffeegenuss direkt von den Tchibo Farmen
title_en: Tchibo Launches Direct-to-Farm Premium Coffees from Brazil and Tanzania
summary_en: Tchibo launched two farm-origin premium coffees (Ipanema espresso from Brazil, Machare filter from Tanzania) priced €13.99–€14.99/500g, emphasizing direct farm ownership, sustainability, and supply-chain transparency. Both farms operate under Tchibo management with focus on traceability and fair conditions.

title: #142: Mach dir Kaffee, keine Sorgen: Der Kaffeereport 2026
title_en: Tchibo 2026 Coffee Report: German Consumer Behavior and Consumption Trends
summary_en: Tchibo's 15th annual coffee report reveals German consumption patterns: 67% of consumers (72% of women) cannot imagine life without daily coffee; 53% drink for enjoyment rather than caffeine; 85% purchase strategically on promotion; 63% maintain home reserves. 47% classify coffee as luxury with 26% spending €13–15.99/kg.

title: Gibt es morgen noch Kaffee? Tchibo ruft zu gemeinsamen Lösungen für die Zukunft von Kaffee und Kakao auf
title_en: Tchibo Accelerates Coffee2Stay: 100% Responsible Sourcing by 2027 as Climate Risk Response
summary_en: Tchibo hosted Berlin dialogue (14 April 2026) with policymakers, NGOs, scientists on climate threats to coffee/cocoa. Company announced expansion of "Coffee2Stay" program targeting 100% responsible raw coffee sourcing by 2027, emphasizing direct farm investment in climate-resilient methods over certification-only approaches. Signals sector-wide supply-chain vulnerability and competitive shift toward direct risk mitigation.

title: The Sustainability Manifesto and the SDGs
title_en: Lavazza Group publishes Sustainability Manifesto aligned with UN SDGs
summary_en: Lavazza Group articulates its sustainability strategy through the Sustainability Manifesto, integrating UN Sustainable Development Goals into all business activities since 2015. The strategy uses 17 SDGs as mandatory prerequisites, with four priority Goals guiding strategy development and 'A Goal in Every Cup' as the reporting framework.

title: Why the actual coffee grounds dominate your cup of coffee's carbon footprint
title_en: Nespresso life-cycle assessment reveals coffee cultivation dominates carbon footprint
summary_en: Nespresso published a comprehensive LCA showing coffee cultivation accounts for nearly 50% of a cup's total carbon footprint. The study demonstrates Nespresso's lungo brewed on Original systems has 24% lower carbon footprint than bean-to-cup machines, and outlines plans to cut GHG emissions 50% by 2030 through scaling regenerative agriculture with 150,000+ coffee farmers.

title: From the ground up: Cultivating gender equality in coffee farming
title_en: Nespresso hosts panel on gender equality in coffee farming
summary_en: Nespresso organized a live panel discussion exploring gender equality in coffee farming communities, covering progress factors, male engagement, and long-term practice building. Panelists included Nespresso gender specialists and external experts from TechnoServe and KIT Royal Tropical Institute.

title: Press Releases 01 Sep 2025 JDE Peet’s share buyback update
title_en: JDE Peet's terminates share buyback program
summary_en: JDE Peet's announced the immediate termination of its share buyback program effective September 1, 2025, having repurchased a total of 5,477,094 ordinary shares at an average price of EUR 20.73 per share for EUR 113.5 million, in compliance with EU Regulation 2016/1052.

title: Press Releases 25 Aug 2025 Keurig Dr Pepper to Acquire JDE Peet’s and Subsequently Separate into Two Independent Companies – a Leading Refreshment Beverage Player and a Global Coffee Champion
title_en: KDP to acquire JDE Peet's and separate into two independent companies
summary_en: Keurig Dr Pepper entered into a definitive agreement to acquire JDE Peet's for EUR 31.85 per share in all-cash transaction (EUR 15.7 billion total equity consideration), creating a global coffee champion through combination of KDP's single-serve leadership with JDE Peet's 300-year heritage and global brand portfolio. Following acquisition closure, KDP will separate into two independent U.S.-listed publicly traded companies: 'Beverage Co.' (North American refreshment beverages, CEO Tim Cofer) and 'Global Coffee Co.' (world's #1 pure-play coffee company, CEO Sudhanshu Priyadarshi), each positioned for focused growth strategies with tailored capital allocation.

title: Press Releases 01 Jul 2025 JDE Peet’s unveils brand-led strategy to accelerate profitable growth and unlock value
title_en: JDE Peet's unveils 'Reignite the Amazing' brand-led strategy
summary_en: JDE Peet's unveiled its 'Reignite the Amazing' brand-led strategy centered on three Big Bets (Peet's, L'OR, and ten local icons led by Jacobs) with a three-phased framework targeting EUR 500 million in net productivity savings by end 2027. Savings will be equally split between reinvestments in high-potential growth initiatives and profit expansion, with disciplined capital allocation and net leverage target improvement to 2x, providing clear strategic direction for sustainable value creation and profitable growth.

title: Press Releases 08 May 2025 JDE Peet’s on track to achieve 2025 outlook with solid Q1 progress; Rob de Groot proposed for appointment to JDE Peet’s Board.
title_en: JDE Peet's reports Q1 2025 progress and strategic portfolio moves
summary_en: JDE Peet's reported Q1 2025 interim update with solid momentum following commercial agreements with majority of customers, with initial volume impacts in January-February rebounding strongly in March. The company divested its tea business in Turkey (EUR 60 million annual sales, negative contribution to adjusted EBIT) to Efor Holding, discontinued L'OR Barista rollout in the US and transferred L'OR capsules business to Peet's, while noting green coffee prices averaged 28% higher in first four months of 2025 compared to H2 2024, prompting consideration of additional price increases.

title: Press Releases 28 Mar 2025 JDE Peet’s receives Fair Pay Leader certification
title_en: JDE Peet's receives Fair Pay Leader certification for gender pay equity
summary_en: JDE Peet's received the Fair Pay Leader gold-standard certification from the Fair Pay Innovation Lab in recognition of its gender pay equity achievements, with a 2024 study of approximately 19,000 employees showing a gender pay gap of 0.52%, well below the 5% threshold anticipated in future EU directives. The certification follows the company's receipt of the 2025 Catalyst Award for advancing workplace inclusion and gender equity.

title: Other 13 Mar 2025 Statement on Product Availability in Certain European Markets
title_en: Statement on Product Availability in Certain European Markets
summary_en: JDE Peet's experienced product availability issues in the Netherlands and Germany in recent weeks, which have been resolved through new retailer agreements covering 90% of global sales. The company attributed supply pressures to doubled green coffee prices caused by extreme weather conditions in producing countries, supply chain disruptions, and macroeconomic factors, while emphasizing its commitment to balancing affordability with quality and sustainable growth.

title: Press Releases 03 Mar 2025 JDE Peet’s starts EUR 250 million share buyback programme
title_en: JDE Peet's Launches EUR 250 Million Share Buyback Programme
summary_en: JDE Peet's announced a EUR 250 million share buyback programme to return capital to shareholders throughout 2025, representing approximately 2.8% of total shares outstanding. Shares will be cancelled or used for share-based employee compensation, executed by an intermediary in compliance with EU Market Abuse Regulation.

title: Press Releases 22 Jan 2025 JDE Peet’s announces changes to its Executive Committee
title_en: JDE Peet's Announces Executive Committee Changes
summary_en: CFO Scott Gray stepped down to relocate to the United States with his family; a new external CFO is to be appointed in Q2 2025 with details disclosed at the February 26, 2025 FY 2024 results announcement. Ricard Barri Valentines was appointed as Chief Marketing Officer effective immediately.

title: Press Releases 12 Dec 2024 JDE Peet’s recognised for Diversity Excellence with 2025 Catalyst Award
title_en: JDE Peet's Receives 2025 Catalyst Award for Diversity Excellence
summary_en: JDE Peet's received the 2025 Catalyst Award recognizing advances in workplace inclusion and gender equity. Since 2021, the company increased women's representation on the Executive Committee from 25% to 36.4%, on the Global Leadership team from 37.2% to 41.5%, and on the Board from 23.1% to 38.5%, supported by its DE&I Maturity Model and Activation Toolkit.

title: Press Releases 21 Oct 2024 JDE Peet’s appoints Rafael Oliveira as Chief Executive Officer
title_en: JDE Peet's Appoints Rafael Oliveira as Chief Executive Officer
summary_en: Rafael Oliveira was appointed CEO effective November 1, 2024, bringing 10 years at Kraft Heinz Company where he served as EVP and President of International Markets (EMEA, APAC, LATAM). His formal appointment is subject to shareholder approval at the June 19, 2025 Annual General Meeting.

title: Press Releases 12 Aug 2024 JDE Peet’s announces interim leadership transition
title_en: JDE Peet's Announces Interim Leadership Transition
summary_en: Luc Vandevelde stepped down as Interim CEO and Chairman due to unforeseen personal matters requiring immediate attention. Peter Harf, JAB Managing Partner and Chairman, assumed the Interim Chairman role, while CFO Scott Gray assumed the additional role of Interim CEO effective immediately.

title: Press Releases 18 Apr 2024 JDE Peet’s signs MOUs with Honduras, Peru and Rwanda to combat coffee-related deforestation
title_en: JDE Peet's Signs MOUs with Honduras, Peru and Rwanda on Coffee Deforestation
summary_en: JDE Peet's expanded its deforestation combat initiative by signing Memoranda of Understanding with Honduras, Peru and Rwanda, joining prior agreements with Ethiopia, PNG, Tanzania and Uganda. The strategy, developed with Enveritas using satellite imagery, AI and on-ground verification, ensures EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) compliance for EU market access and supports smallholder farmer resilience through reforestation and regenerative agricultural practices.

title: Press Releases 28 Mar 2024 JDE Peet’s commits to newly validated near-term and net-zero SBTi targets
title_en: JDE Peet's Commits to Science-Based Climate Targets (SBTi Net-Zero)
summary_en: JDE Peet's committed to validated Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) targets aligned with 1.5°C pathway: 43.3% reduction in scope 1&2 GHG emissions by 2030, 25% reduction in scope 3 emissions by 2030, 30.3% reduction in Forest/Land/Agriculture emissions by 2030, and zero deforestation across coffee, pulp, palm and cocoa by December 31, 2025. The company achieved 21% scope 1&2 and 9% scope 3 reductions since 2023.

title: M&A 26 Mar 2024 JDE Peet’s completes transaction to manufacture, market and sell Caribou-branded consumer and foodservice coffee products
title_en: JDE Peet's Completes Caribou Coffee Transaction
summary_en: JDE Peet's completed acquisition of Caribou's coffee roasting operations in Minneapolis and a global license to manufacture, market and sell Caribou consumer and foodservice coffee products. The transaction includes a long-term supply arrangement for Caribou's 800+ coffeehouses in 11 countries, expanding JDE Peet's premium portfolio in the US market.

title: Press Releases 22 Feb 2024 L’OR Espresso enters into global partnership with Ferrari
title_en: L'OR Espresso Partners with Ferrari in FIA World Endurance Championship
summary_en: L'OR Espresso announced a global brand partnership with Ferrari for the FIA World Endurance Championship, including the 24 Hours of Le Mans. The collaboration features branding on the Ferrari 499P Hypercar and driver uniforms, bespoke content creation, L'OR service in team hospitality, and a planned limited-edition coffee and brewer product range inspired by both brands.

title: Press Releases 16 Feb 2024 JDE Peet’s expands collaboration with Costa Coffee
title_en: JDE Peet's Expands Costa Coffee Collaboration in Great Britain
summary_en: JDE Peet's will manufacture, distribute and sell Costa Coffee branded aluminium capsules for the L'OR Barista brewer system in Great Britain beginning September 2024. The expanded partnership leverages Costa's premium coffee shop heritage and JDE Peet's in-home espresso technology to drive premiumisation and category growth, while other Costa products remain under Costa ownership.

title: Press Releases 13 Feb 2024 JDE Peet’s leads global effort to fight coffee-related deforestation
title_en: JDE Peet's Leads Global Deforestation Assessment Initiative
summary_en: JDE Peet's and Enveritas announced a global deforestation assessment programme using satellite imagery, AI and on-ground verification to measure coffee-related deforestation. Initial Memoranda of Understanding signed with Ethiopia, PNG, Tanzania and Uganda aim to ensure coffee is grown on land not deforested after 2020, with JDE Peet's supporting reforestation if non-compliance is found, fully aligned with the EU Deforestation Regulation.

title: Press Releases 09 Jan 2024 JDE Peet's recognised as sustainability leader by 2023 Dow Jones Sustainability Europe Index, only coffee company featured on list
title_en: JDE Peet's recognized as only coffee company in 2023 Dow Jones Sustainability Europe Index
summary_en: JDE Peet's was included in the prestigious Dow Jones Sustainability Europe Index as the only coffee company on the list, among four European food companies total. The recognition reflects substantially improved CSA scores and the company's commitment to responsible sourcing, minimizing footprint, and connecting people through its Common Grounds Programme.

title: Press Releases 28 Nov 2023 JDE Peet’s prices EUR 1 billion dual-tranche bond issue
title_en: JDE Peet's prices €1 billion dual-tranche bond issue
summary_en: JDE Peet's issued €1 billion in bonds comprising two tranches: €500 million at 4.125% due 2030 and €500 million at 4.500% due 2034. The senior unsecured notes, listed on Luxembourg Stock Exchange's EuroMTF, will be used for general corporate purposes and refinancing existing debt.

title: Press Releases 19 Jul 2023 JDE Peet’s accelerates its sustainability agenda with a breakthrough paper pack for soluble coffee
title_en: JDE Peet's launches breakthrough recyclable paper pack for soluble coffee
summary_en: JDE Peet's announced plans to launch a first-in-market recyclable paper pack for soluble coffee, designed to incentivize reuse of existing glass jars and tin formats while achieving the lowest carbon footprint within JDE's product range. Available from 2024 across multiple brands and markets, supporting the company's goal of 100% reusable/recyclable/compostable packaging by 2030.

title: Press Releases 24 Apr 2026 JDE PEET’S N.V. announces consent solicitations in respect of outstanding Euro Notes
title_en: JDE Peet's Announces Consent Solicitations for EUR 3.35B Note Restructuring
summary_en: On 24 April 2026, JDE Peet's N.V. announced separate consent solicitations to modify six series of outstanding Euro Notes (EUR 3.35B total, 2027–2034 maturities) to align with the new corporate structure following KDP's 97.75% acquisition stake. The company will convert to a private B.V., delist from Euronext Amsterdam on 30 April 2026, execute a post-closing demerger as of 1 May 2026, and introduce new guarantors to the debt structure.

title: Press Releases 22 Apr 2026 Coffee sector leaders launch industry-first global mapping initiative to accelerate the transition towards a deforestation-free coffee sector
title_en: Coffee Canopy Partnership – Global Deforestation Mapping Initiative
summary_en: On 22 April 2026, JDE Peet's (now part of KDP) launched the Coffee Canopy Partnership with leading coffee companies (Louis Dreyfus Company, Sucden, Neumann Kaffee Gruppe, Touton, Sucafina, Tchibo) to create the first comprehensive, openly accessible global map of coffee production using Airbus satellite technology. The East Africa pilot covers 1.2 million km² across Ethiopia, Tanzania, Kenya, Uganda, Burundi, and Rwanda (supported by UK FCDO), with plans for worldwide coverage by end-2027.

title: Press Releases 13 Apr 2026 Keurig Dr Pepper Announces Results of Post-Closing Acceptance Period for Offer for JDE Peet’s - 97.75% of all Shares tendered
title_en: KDP Acquisition of JDE Peet's – Post-Closing Acceptance Period Results
summary_en: On 13 April 2026, Keurig Dr Pepper announced that during the post-closing acceptance period, an additional 7.82 million JDE Peet's shares were tendered (1.61% of total), bringing KDP's combined stake to 474.53 million shares (97.75% of all outstanding shares). KDP will proceed with statutory buyout proceedings to acquire the remaining 2.25% of shares.

title: Press Releases 01 Apr 2026 Keurig Dr Pepper Acquires JDE Peet’s and Announces Rafael Oliveira as CEO of Future Global Coffee Co.
title_en: KDP Acquisition Completion – Rafael Oliveira Appointed CEO of Global Coffee Co.
summary_en: On 1 April 2026, Keurig Dr Pepper announced successful completion of its acquisition of JDE Peet's with 96.22% share acceptance. KDP appointed Rafael Oliveira (Brazilian-British, former Kraft Heinz EMEA/APAC/LATAM president, Goldman Sachs banker) as CEO of KDP's Coffee Operating Unit and designated future CEO of the independent Global Coffee Co. post-separation.

title: Press Releases 27 Mar 2026 Keurig Dr Pepper declares offer for JDE Peet’s unconditional - 96.22% of all Shares tendered - Settlement Date will be 1 April 2026
title_en: KDP Declares JDE Peet's Offer Unconditional – 96.22% Acceptance Achieved
summary_en: On 27 March 2026, Keurig Dr Pepper declared its public cash offer for JDE Peet's unconditional after 96.22% of all shares (466.7 million) were tendered during the offer period, satisfying all conditions precedent. Settlement occurred on 1 April 2026, with a post-closing acceptance period (30 March – 13 April 2026) for remaining shareholders.

title: Press Releases 02 Mar 2026 JDE Peet’s EGM adopts all resolutions in relation to KDP Offer
title_en: JDE Peet's EGM Approves KDP Acquisition and Post-Closing Restructuring
summary_en: On 2 March 2026, JDE Peet's Extraordinary General Meeting approved all proposals related to the KDP acquisition, including post-closing restructuring measures, board appointments, amendments to Articles of Association, and discharge of resigning directors. The EGM also reduced the Acceptance Threshold from 95% to 80% of outstanding capital, facilitating KDP's path to 100% ownership.

title: Press Releases 24 Feb 2026 JDE Peet’s reports full-year results 2025
title_en: JDE Peet's reports full-year results 2025
summary_en: JDE Peet's delivered strong financial performance in 2025 with organic sales growth of 15.3%, driven by 19.5% pricing, partially offset by 4.3% volume/mix decline. Organic adjusted EBIT grew 1.2% to EUR 1.3 billion, with free cash flow of EUR 1.13 billion and net leverage at 2.3x, demonstrating resilience despite unprecedented green coffee inflation. The company made significant progress on its 'Reignite the Amazing' brand-led strategy, with the KDP acquisition expected to close in early Q2 2026.

title: Press Releases 29 Jan 2026 JDE Peet’s launches Nature Transition Plan to advance regenerative agriculture and deforestation-free coffee supply chains
title_en: JDE Peet's launches Nature Transition Plan aligned with TNFD and global frameworks
summary_en: JDE Peet's became the first food and beverage company to launch a nature transition plan fully aligned with the Taskforce on Nature-related Financial Disclosures (TNFD), Global Biodiversity Framework, and Science-Based Targets for Nature. The plan commits to deforestation-free supply chains beyond EU Deforestation Regulation compliance, expanding regenerative agriculture to 200,000 hectares by 2030, and achieving 100% responsibly sourced green coffee by 2028, building on a decade-long Common Grounds program reaching nearly one million farmers across 29 countries.

title: Press Releases 04 Dec 2025 JDE Peet’s prices EUR 600 million senior unsecured notes issue
title_en: JDE Peet's prices EUR 600 million senior unsecured notes
summary_en: JDE Peet's issued EUR 600 million in senior unsecured floating rate notes due December 2027, priced at 3M EURIBOR + 70 basis points, under its EUR 5 billion debt issuance programme. The notes, listed on the Euro MTF market of the Luxembourg Stock Exchange, are intended for general corporate purposes and refinancing of upcoming debt maturities.

title: Press Releases 27 Oct 2025 JDE Peet’s reports strategic and operational progress - Confirms 2025 outlook
title_en: JDE Peet's reports Q3 2025 strategic and operational progress
summary_en: JDE Peet's completed integration of its US capsules business into Peet's and progressed its 'Reignite the Amazing' strategy with significant operational changes including US distribution model transition (DSD to direct central distribution), exit from low-margin Food Ingredients business in Asia, and plant closures in Northeast Brazil and Northeast US. The company completed approximately 96% of its second wave of global price negotiations and terminated its share buyback program, while advancing toward the KDP acquisition closure expected in H1 2026.

title: Press Releases 14 Oct 2025 JDE Peet’s unveils transformed Innovation Laboratory in Utrecht to accelerate next-generation coffee breakthroughs
title_en: JDE Peet's unveils transformed Innovation Laboratory in Utrecht
summary_en: JDE Peet's opened a fully revamped modular Innovation Laboratory in Utrecht, Netherlands, as a strategic expansion of its R&D capabilities, complemented by a new facility in Joure with combined investment of EUR 8 million. The laboratory focuses on developing next-generation coffee products in single-serve, capsules, ready-to-drink, and instant formats alongside sustainable packaging, with advanced heating and ventilation systems designed to recycle heat and reduce energy consumption.

title: Press Releases 01 Oct 2025 JDE Peet’s calls for coffee industry to implement Regenerative Agriculture Coffee Roadmap to boost coffee supply chain resilience
title_en: JDE Peet's calls for industry-wide Regenerative Agriculture Coffee Roadmap implementation
summary_en: Marking ten years of its Common Grounds farmer programs, JDE Peet's called on the global coffee industry to implement the Regenerative Agriculture Coffee Roadmap to address worsening climate conditions and secure supply chain resilience. The company reported reaching nearly 835,000 farmers across 29 countries, implementing regenerative agricultural practices on 50% of covered farms, and achieving 83.2% responsibly sourced green coffee globally, with 100% responsibly sourced in Europe.

title: Press Releases 19 Sep 2025 Update on intended recommended public offer by KDP for JDE Peet's
title_en: Update on KDP's intended recommended public offer for JDE Peet's
summary_en: KDP and JDE Peet's provided a joint progress update on the intended recommended all-cash public offer at EUR 31.85 per share (33% premium to 90-day VWAP), totaling EUR 15.7 billion equity consideration, with JDE Peet's also paying a previously declared dividend of EUR 0.36 per share. The parties confirmed expected submission of the offer memorandum to the Dutch AFM by November 16, 2025, with transaction closure anticipated in H1 2026 subject to satisfaction or waiver of customary closing conditions.

title: Read more JDE Peet's notice of results of consent solicitations in respect of outstanding euro notes
title_en: JDE Peet's Consent Solicitations Results – EUR 3.35B Euro Notes Restructuring
summary_en: On 18 May 2026, JDE Peet's announced results of consent solicitations to modify terms of EUR 3.35 billion in outstanding Notes (six series: EUR 600M 2027, EUR 600M 2028, EUR 750M 2029, EUR 500M 2030, EUR 500M 2033, EUR 500M 2034) to reflect the company's new corporate structure post-KDP acquisition and planned separation. The amendments introduce new guarantors under the post-closing corporate hierarchy.

title: More News > (THE LATEST COFFEE AND TEA NEWS)
title_en: JDE Peet's Newsroom – Acquisition Integration with KDP
summary_en: JDE Peet's newsroom confirmed that following Keurig Dr Pepper's acquisition completed in April 2026, JDE Peet's now operates as part of KDP's Coffee Operating Unit and directed readers to the transaction announcement for additional details.

title: BGBl. 2026 I Nr. 207
title_en: German Packaging Law Amendment Implementing EU Regulation 2025/40
summary_en: Germany enacted federal law (BGBl. 2026 I Nr. 207) effective July 13, 2026, adapting packaging regulations to EU Regulation (EU) 2025/40. This regulation establishes extended producer responsibility requirements and packaging standards that directly affect product packaging, labeling, and distribution compliance for beverage manufacturers across the EU/EEA, including JDE Peet's coffee and tea products sold in Germany and affiliated markets.

title: Ocean capacity is rising, demand is cooling, and ‘the market is starting to turn’
title_en: Container shipping rates decline as market emerges from peak season
summary_en: Container spot freight rates on major east-west ocean trades recorded their first decline since end of April, signalling market shift from peak-season pricing. Shanghai-Rotterdam dropped 1% to $4,873 per 40ft, while Shanghai-Genoa fell 3% to $6,300 per 40ft, per Drewry's World Container Index.

title: AI cargo props up air cargo market amid warnings of an early peak
title_en: AI infrastructure demand drives air freight rates 33% higher year-on-year
summary_en: Global air cargo rates rose 33% year-on-year in June 2026 with volumes up 9%, driven by AI infrastructure and semiconductor shipments despite weaker ecommerce demand. Middle Eastern carrier capacity constraints and geopolitical risks pose risks of early peak season, per Flexport analysis.

title: Sea Legend Shipping returns to Arctic route with China-Europe NSR sailings
title_en: Chinese operator resumes Arctic Northern Sea Route sailings China-Europe
summary_en: Sea Legend Shipping scheduled eight sailings via the Northern Sea Route between August and October 2026 from Ningbo to Felixstowe, completing voyage in 21 days. Route expansion reflects growing use of Arctic passage as alternative to traditional Suez-based Asia-Europe services.

title: Forwarders say policy not consumers will determine green transition
title_en: Logistics industry: policy, not consumer demand, will drive green supply chains
summary_en: Freight forwarders argue that legislative policy—not consumer behaviour shifts—will determine supply chain decarbonization. Economic uncertainty and ecommerce growth indicate governments must mandate cleaner logistics rather than rely on voluntary consumer purchasing decisions.

title: Uber preia Delivery Hero, proprietarul Glovo, într-o tranzacție de 13 miliarde de euro
title_en: Uber Acquires Delivery Hero for €13 Billion, Including Glovo Operations Across EU/EEA
summary_en: Uber has agreed to acquire Delivery Hero in a €13 billion transaction, taking control of Glovo and related delivery operations spanning 14 countries, including Romania, Spain, Portugal, Poland, Italy, Bulgaria, and Croatia. Concurrently, Delivery Hero is divesting South American and selected European operations (valued at €1.4 billion) to investment fund SSW Partners.

title: Kuehne + Nagel rolls out cloud-native contract logistics solution
title_en: Kuehne+Nagel launches AI-enabled warehouse platform across 1,000+ global sites
summary_en: Swiss logistics provider Kuehne+Nagel evolved its KN SwiftLOG system into a cloud-native, agentic AI-enabled warehouse management platform built on Blue Yonder technology. Rollout spans 1,000+ contract logistics sites globally to drive efficiency and resource optimisation.

title: Seven & i Shares Jump 3% On Żabka Convenience Store Stake Talks
title_en: Seven & i Holdings Stock Price Rises on Żabka Investment Announcement
summary_en: Seven & i Holdings' share price increased 3% on the Tokyo stock exchange following the company's announcement of advanced negotiations to acquire a stake in Żabka Group. The market reaction reflects investor confidence in Seven & i's strategic expansion into Eastern European convenience retail, affirming the strategic value of strengthening distribution channels in the EU convenience segment.

title: EU electrification push must go renewable and cut energy waste
title_en: EU Electrification Plan Prioritizes Renewable Energy and Energy Waste Reduction
summary_en: The European Commission has announced an electrification plan emphasizing transition to 100% renewable energy and energy waste reduction. This regulatory framework will directly affect manufacturing and operational costs for JDE Peet's facilities across the EU/EEA as the beverage production industry transitions to renewable energy sources.

title: Why Seven & i's Investment Could Be Valuable For Żabka: Bernstein Analysis
title_en: Seven & i Holdings in Advanced Negotiations to Invest in Polish Convenience Retailer Żabka
summary_en: Japan's Seven & i Holdings (parent company of 7-Eleven, a leading global convenience retail operator) is in talks to acquire a strategic stake in Żabka Group, a major Polish convenience store chain with significant presence across Central and Eastern Europe. This investment represents Seven & i's accelerated expansion of European convenience retail operations, a critical distribution channel for beverage brands including coffee and tea products.

title: Marking its own homework, or a genuine attempt to reform? The EC reviews the Foreign Subsidies Regulation
title_en: European Commission Foreign Subsidies Regulation Review: Proposed Compliance and Enforcement Reforms
summary_en: The European Commission is reviewing the Foreign Subsidies Regulation to propose reforms that reduce compliance burdens while strengthening enforcement across the EU. These changes will reshape how EU competition authorities evaluate subsidies and state aid that distort the internal market, with potential implications for competitive dynamics in sectors where JDE Peet's operates.

title: Coffee futures down sharply after Cecafé’s positive data on Brazilian exports, Illy focuses on AI applied to regenerative agriculture plans
title_en: Coffee commodity prices decline on improved Brazilian export outlook
summary_en: ICE Arabica futures fell 4.3% on strong Brazilian export data from Cecafé, indicating improved supply conditions and downward commodity price pressure. Competitor Illy announced AI-driven regenerative agriculture initiatives, reflecting industry focus on sustainable sourcing practices amid evolving global supply chain dynamics.

title: Nims, a Lavazza Group company, grew well in 2025: revenues at 132.5 million euros and over 301 million capsules on direct sale
title_en: Lavazza subsidiary Nims reports strong growth in direct-to-consumer capsule sales
summary_en: Nims, a Lavazza Group subsidiary specializing in direct-sale coffee capsules and water filtration systems, reported €132.5 million in revenue for 2025 (up 6% year-on-year) with 301 million capsules sold directly to 40,000 customers. The performance demonstrates strong commercial momentum in the EU direct-to-consumer capsule segment, where it competes with established players in the premium packaged coffee market.

title: SLI and e-Smart bet on electric aircraft leasing to unlock regional cargo market
title_en: Operating lease removes financing barrier to electric cargo aircraft deployment
summary_en: Space Leasing International signed an operating lease for up to 20 BETA Technologies ALIA electric cargo aircraft with e-Smart Group, converting prior purchase order into lease structure. Financing innovation could accelerate commercial rollout of zero-emission regional air cargo capacity.

title: PPWR: Where are we now, and how must we adapt?
title_en: PPWR: Where Are We Now and How Must We Adapt?
summary_en: As the Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation (PPWR) approaches enforcement, industry analysis examines key requirements and identifies areas of remaining uncertainty. The report outlines compliance obligations and implementation challenges for manufacturers across the EU.

title: Clear-up after toxic gas leak sees some Antwerp quays reopen
title_en: Antwerp port partially resumes after hydrofluoric acid gas leak hospitalizes 155
summary_en: Port of Antwerp's Deurganckdok area partially reopened after emergency cleanup from hydrofluoric acid toxic gas leak that hospitalized 155 people. Northern MSC-PSA MEPT terminal quay remains closed, while DP World's Antwerp Gateway and adjacent quays resumed operations.

title: Cots, cows, and the fight for clean water
title_en: EU Drinking Water Safety Standards Under Review for Modernization
summary_en: Advocates have highlighted that the EU's drinking water safety standards remain unchanged from the analog television era and fail to reflect modern science. Updates to these standards will directly impact water quality requirements and manufacturing compliance obligations for JDE Peet's beverage production facilities throughout the EU.

title: Europska komisija otvorila javno savjetovanje o neopravdanim teritorijalnim ograničenjima opskrbe
title_en: European Commission Opens Public Consultation on Territorial Supply Constraints
summary_en: The European Commission has launched a public consultation on measures to address unjustified territorial supply constraints (TSC)—business practices where manufacturers restrict retailers from purchasing products in one EU member state and reselling them in another. Such restrictions can limit cross-border trade, reduce consumer choice, and contribute to price disparities across the EU.

title: Uber In €12.9bn Deal For Delivery Hero To Create Global Takeout Giant
title_en: Uber Launches €12.9 Billion Public Takeover Offer for German Food Delivery Company Delivery Hero
summary_en: Uber launched a public takeover offer for Delivery Hero, a German-based food delivery platform, valuing the company at €12.9 billion (approximately $14.8 billion USD). The proposed acquisition would consolidate Uber Eats with Delivery Hero's European and global operations to create an integrated food delivery and quick-service restaurant platform, significantly affecting the distribution channels for beverage consumption in the out-of-home dining and delivery segment.

title: Analiză TPC Concept: 60-70% dintre produse generează doar 5% din vânzări
title_en: Retail Portfolio Analysis: 60-70% of SKUs Generate Only 5% of Sales
summary_en: An analysis by TPC Concept reveals that despite declining consumer purchasing power in Romania, retailers continue to expand product portfolios to offset slower consumption. However, approximately 60-70% of products in a typical retailer's assortment generate only 5% of total sales while consuming disproportionate resources in inventory, logistics, marketing, and administration.

title: Carrefour România a lansat un nou serviciu de asistență la cumpărături
title_en: Carrefour Romania Launches Free In-Store Shopping Assistance Service
summary_en: Carrefour Romania has introduced a new daily free shopping assistance service available across all hypermarket and market-format locations. The initiative aims to enhance customer experience and streamline purchase decision-making.

title: Wolt: În serile de fotbal, românii au comandat din retail căpșuni și băuturi carbogazoase
title_en: Wolt Delivery Data: Carbonated Beverages Surge During Football Season in Romania
summary_en: Wolt platform data from Romania's international football season reveals significant spikes in retail orders during match evenings, particularly for carbonated beverages (up 1,100%) and sweet snacks, alongside restaurant orders from consumers preparing for matches.

title: Nestlé Spain Converts Coffee Capsules Into Outdoor Furniture
title_en: Nestlé Spain Converts Coffee Capsules Into Outdoor Furniture
summary_en: Nestlé Professional Spain launched a circular economy initiative converting used coffee capsules and coffee grounds into outdoor terrace furniture. This initiative in the coffee capsule market segment is directly relevant to JDE Peet's competing Senseo and Tassimo capsule systems.

title: Consumatorii țin cont de sănătate și starea de bine în deciziile de zi cu zi. Ce cumpără, ce vor și în cine au încredere?
title_en: Consumers Increasingly Factor Health and Wellness Into Daily Purchasing Decisions
summary_en: A PwC analysis demonstrates that Romanian consumers prioritize health and wellness when making purchasing decisions. The study examines consumer buying patterns, preferences, and brand trust, reflecting a broader market shift toward health-conscious consumption that affects beverage category demand.

title: Alimentele, cu aproape 6% mai scumpe în vara acestui an față de vara anului trecut
title_en: Food Prices Rise 6% in Summer 2026 Versus Prior Year
summary_en: Food prices in Romania have increased by approximately 6% in summer 2026 compared to summer 2025, reflecting persistent inflation across the FMCG sector. This price escalation directly affects input costs, production margins, and consumer purchasing power for beverages and packaged foods.

title: Iced Tea Finally Breaks the Sugar Spell
title_en: Iced Tea Finally Breaks the Sugar Spell
summary_en: The ready-to-drink (RTD) tea category is undergoing significant market shift away from sugar-laden products toward healthier options. Legacy brands historically positioned tea as a soft drink rather than authentic iced tea, but consumer preferences now favor lower-sugar, more authentic beverage formulations.

title: Sri Lanka Rapidly Shifts Toward RTD Tea and Re-Exports
title_en: Sri Lanka Rapidly Shifts Toward RTD Tea and Re-Exports
summary_en: Sri Lanka's tea export sector is undergoing structural transformation, with traditional bulk and packet tea volumes declining while ready-to-drink (RTD) tea and re-export segments expand rapidly. This shift reflects broader market evolution toward value-added, convenience-oriented tea products.

title: Indian Tea Exports Reach Record High
title_en: Indian Tea Exports Reach Record High
summary_en: India achieved an all-time export high for tea in 2025, demonstrating economic resilience and strong global demand dynamics. The record volume represents significant supply availability in global tea markets serving major multinational tea manufacturers.

title: The Global Coffee Price Paradox
title_en: The Global Coffee Price Paradox
summary_en: The global coffee sector faces a historic paradox: four of the world's leading coffee exporters are expected to reach unprecedented production volumes simultaneously. This supply surge creates significant commodity pricing implications with direct impact on coffee manufacturers' cost structures and market dynamics.

title: Will Sustainable Coffee Pods Finally Take Over
title_en: Will Sustainable Coffee Pods Finally Take Over
summary_en: Single-serve coffee pods face intensifying sustainability scrutiny despite strong consumer demand for convenience. The article examines whether environmental concerns and circular economy pressures will reshape the pod-based coffee market, directly addressing key challenges for Senseo and Tassimo systems.

title: Comisia Europeană dă în judecată Ungaria din cauza plafonării marjei comerciale pentru alimente
title_en: EU Commission Takes Hungary to Court Over Food Profit Margin Caps
summary_en: The European Commission has filed legal action against Hungary for implementing caps on profit margins for food products. Coffee and tea fall within this scope, so the regulation directly affects JDE Peet's pricing strategy and distribution margins in Hungary and may influence similar policies across other EU member states.

title: German corporate fines reform: internal investigations and compliance frameworks as key mitigating factors
title_en: German corporate fines reform: internal investigations and compliance frameworks as key mitigating factors
summary_en: Germany is recalibrating its corporate fines regime through new draft sentencing guidelines. Internal investigations, remediation measures, and robust compliance frameworks are being positioned as central mitigating factors in corporate fine assessments, elevating compliance governance as a strategic priority for regulated entities.

title: UK industrial action reform: workers gain new detriment protection
title_en: UK industrial action reform: workers gain new detriment protection
summary_en: From October 30, 2026, UK employment law will expand detriment protection for workers participating in lawful industrial action. Employers will face broader liability if workers are subjected to unfavorable treatment during such actions, strengthening labor protections in the UK market.

title: ECF Logistic Committee (in person, Stockholm)
title_en: European Coffee Federation Logistic Committee Meeting (Stockholm)
summary_en: The European Coffee Federation held an in-person logistic committee meeting in Stockholm. The event was open to ECF members only; substantive agenda details are not publicly available.

title: ECF Executive Committee (online)
title_en: European Coffee Federation Executive Committee Meeting
summary_en: The European Coffee Federation held an online executive committee meeting. The session was open to ECF members only; specific agenda items and decisions are not publicly detailed.

title: ECF Technical & Regulatory Committee (in person – Brussels)
title_en: European Coffee Federation Technical & Regulatory Committee Meeting (Brussels)
summary_en: The European Coffee Federation held an in-person technical and regulatory committee meeting in Brussels. Substantive agenda details are available to ECF members only.

title: ECF Contaminants Committee (in person – Brussels)
title_en: European Coffee Federation Contaminants Committee Meeting (Brussels)
summary_en: The European Coffee Federation held an in-person contaminants committee meeting in Brussels. Details are restricted to ECF members.

title: ECF at World of Coffee Brussels 2026
title_en: ECF Participation in World of Coffee Brussels 2026
summary_en: The European Coffee Federation participated in the World of Coffee Brussels event (25–27 June 2026) with public and closed-door sessions on coffee policy and global value chains. ECF co-hosted a public discussion with the International Coffee Organization (ICO) highlighting coffee's economic contribution to Europe. The federation engaged on industry policy matters affecting the coffee sector.

title: Review of the EU Merger Guidelines: BEUC’s response to the public consultation
title_en: EU Merger Guidelines Under Review: Consumer Protection and Market Concentration Focus
summary_en: The European Consumer Organisation (BEUC) submitted its response to the European Commission's public consultation on revising the EU Merger Guidelines. BEUC advocates for maintaining the guidelines' focus on protecting competitive markets and consumer welfare, opposing industrial or political objectives that undermine competition enforcement. Market concentration in consumer-facing sectors including food and beverages has increased significantly in recent years, resulting in higher prices and reduced consumer choice, making robust merger control essential.

title: New Report Highlights Coffee’s Shared Value Chain Linking Producing Countries and Europe
title_en: ECF Report: Coffee's Shared Value Chain Linking Producing Countries and Europe
summary_en: The European Coffee Federation, in collaboration with Europe Economics, published a report demonstrating coffee's economic contribution to both producing countries and Europe. The report notes that approximately 12.5 million farming households globally depend on coffee for livelihoods (around 85% in producing regions) while the industry generates significant jobs and economic activity across Europe. The report emphasizes the interconnected nature of the coffee value chain.

title: Støtter europeisk initiativ for bedre kontroll med oppkjøp
title_en: Norway backs European merger reporting framework expansion
summary_en: Norwegian competition authority endorses a common European recommendation for national competition authorities to have expanded powers to impose mandatory merger reporting. This reporting requirement is already established in Norwegian competition law and reflects a broader trend across Europe toward enhanced merger control oversight.

title: AZTN kaznio KAUFLAND HRVATSKA k.d., Zagreb s 300.000,00 eura zbog nametanja nepoštenih trgovačkih praksi
title_en: Croatian Competition Authority Fines Kaufland €300,000 for Unfair Trading Practices in Food Supply Chain
summary_en:
The Croatian Competition Agency (AZTN) imposed a fine of €300,000 on Kaufland Hrvatska k.d., Zagreb for severe violations of the Law on Prohibition of Unfair Trading Practices in the Food Supply Chain. The decision followed an ex officio administrative procedure to determine whether Kaufland engaged in unfair commercial practices as a buyer in food distribution.

This enforcement action directly impacts supply chain relationships in Central Europe, where Kaufland operates as a major multi-country retailer with significant purchasing power. The fine signals AZTN's commitment to protecting supplier interests against leveraging by large retailers and signals enforcement risk for food and beverage suppliers, including coffee and tea manufacturers, dealing with major supermarket chains across the region.

title: ECF and UNIDO Sign Joint Declaration on Sustainable African Coffee Value Chains
title_en: ECF and UNIDO Joint Declaration on Sustainable African Coffee Value Chains
summary_en: The European Coffee Federation and the United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) signed a Joint Declaration on sustainable African coffee value chain transformation at UN House Brussels. The declaration formalizes a structured public-private partnership with participation from the European Commission's DG INTPA and EU Member States. The initiative aims to support sustainable transformation across African coffee production and export infrastructure.

title: East Africa’s Coffee Renaissance
title_en: East Africa's Coffee Renaissance
summary_en: East Africa remains pivotal in global coffee culture, with Ethiopia as the birthplace of arabica coffee and Uganda as a cornerstone of robusta production. The article explores production renaissance and supply dynamics in these strategically important coffee-sourcing regions.

title: The Coffee Barometer Assess 20 Years of Sustainability Efforts
title_en: The Coffee Barometer: 20 Years of Sustainability Efforts Assessment
summary_en: The coffee sector continues to face significant sustainability challenges, including endemic poverty among producers and the escalating impacts of climate change. The 20-year barometer assessment evaluates ongoing progress in addressing these structural issues within the global coffee industry.

title: ECF and ECA publish Joint Industry Specification for jute bags and jute yarns in cocoa and coffee supply chains
title_en: New Joint Industry Specification for Jute Bags in Coffee and Cocoa Supply Chains
summary_en: On 17 June 2026, the European Coffee Federation and European Cocoa Association jointly published a new Industry Specification for jute bags and jute yarns used as food-contact packaging in coffee and cocoa supply chains. The specification provides a modern alternative to the IJO98/01 standard that has governed jute bag production for decades, addressing updated packaging requirements and food safety standards for the sector.

title: Daglegvare: Forskjellar i innkjøpsprisar nær uendra frå fjoråret
title_en: Norwegian grocery chains' purchasing price disparities stable in 2025
summary_en: Norwegian competition authority's annual monitoring of major grocery chains' purchasing prices shows price differences between chains remain nearly unchanged from 2024. The authority has tracked these purchasing disparities since 2017 as ongoing oversight of Norway's grocery retail market structure and competitive dynamics.

title: EU Parliament backs EU-US deal on tariffs, oil and gas
title_en: EU Parliament Approves EU-US Trade Deal with Reduced Tariffs
summary_en: The European Parliament has voted to support an EU-US trade agreement that eliminates EU tariffs on US goods in exchange for a US tariff cap of 15%. This deal directly impacts KDP's (parent company of JDE Peet's) supply chain efficiency, import/export costs, and competitive positioning across the European market.

title: Diving deeper into the EU's latest guidance on the PPWR
title_en: EU Commission Guidance on PPWR Implementation
summary_en: On 5 June 2026, the European Commission published a Commission Notice providing detailed clarification on the Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation in practice. This official guidance document addresses implementation questions and provides mandatory direction for manufacturers and stakeholders navigating PPWR compliance.

title: JOINT STATEMENT- Towards pragmatic and harmonised labelling in the EU
title_en: Joint Statement: Pragmatic and Harmonised Packaging Labelling in the EU
summary_en: The European Coffee Federation, alongside 15 leading consumer goods associations, has issued a joint statement to the European Commission and Member States calling for a pragmatic approach to harmonised packaging labelling under the Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation (PPWR). The industry seeks to prevent single-market fragmentation and preserve competitiveness in consumer goods manufacturing.

title: PPWR timeline: the key dates to know
title_en: PPWR Timeline: Key Enforcement Dates and Milestones
summary_en: The European Organisation for Packaging and the Environment (Europen) has published a comprehensive timeline of Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation requirements, including enforcement dates, relevant Articles, and guidance recommendations. The timeline provides critical compliance deadlines for manufacturers planning PPWR implementation.

title: ROUNDTABLE: Sustainable Coffee Value Chains in Africa: A Public-Private Sector Dialogue
title_en: UNIDO Roundtable: Sustainable Coffee Value Chains in Africa
summary_en: On 24 June 2026, UNIDO, in partnership with the European Coffee Federation, convened a high-level roundtable at the UN House to explore how public-private partnerships and technical assistance can drive sustainable investment across African coffee value chains. The dialogue brought together private-sector leaders, EU officials, and international organizations to address sustainability in coffee sourcing.

title: High-level dialogue on EUDR with coffee producing countries by ICO, IACO & ECF
title_en: High-level Dialogue on EUDR with Coffee-Producing Countries
summary_en: On 25 June 2026, at World of Coffee in Brussels, the European Coffee Federation participated in a high-level dialogue on the EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) alongside the International Coffee Organization and Inter-African Coffee Organisation. The session brought together coffee-producing countries and European industry representatives to address EUDR compliance and implementation challenges.

title: Kronikk: Effektiv konkurranse forutsetter god handelsskikk
title_en: Effective competition requires fair commercial practices in grocery supply chains
summary_en: Norwegian competition authority discusses how good commercial practice law supports effective competition by establishing requirements for written agreements, protection against imitation, and investment protection between suppliers and retailers. These legal protections give suppliers confidence to make long-term investments and develop new products for Norwegian markets.

title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Impacts Global Coffee Industry
title_en: Strait of Hormuz Blockade Disrupts Global Coffee Supply Chains
summary_en: Blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has disrupted global supply chains including coffee logistics. This critical maritime chokepoint affects shipping routes and delivery timelines for coffee importers worldwide, including European operators.

title: Convenience Coffee Takes Over
title_en: Convenience Coffee Format Expansion and Market Trends
summary_en: The coffee industry continues rapid evolution toward convenience formats as standalone purchase categories. This trend directly reflects market demand for instant, capsule, and ready-to-drink formats central to JDE Peet's portfolio strategy.

title: Letter: the EU Fertiliser Action Plan must herald the end of fossil input dependency
title_en: EU Fertiliser Action Plan Must Herald End of Fossil Input Dependency
summary_en: Over 70 organisations urge the European Commission to prioritise agroecology and organic farming in the Fertiliser Action Plan. EU agricultural policy direction shapes farming practices in coffee/tea supply regions serving European markets.

title: Beyond Compliance: The Future of Coffee Supply Chains
title_en: Coffee Supply Chain Sustainability: Moving Beyond Minimum Compliance
summary_en: The coffee industry faces mounting regulatory and stakeholder pressure to deliver genuine sustainability practices in supply chains rather than minimum compliance. This trend reflects EU regulatory expectations and market demands directly affecting coffee sourcing and operations.

title: Regen Tea Program Enters Pilot Phase
title_en: Regenerative Agriculture Pilot Program for Tea Cultivation
summary_en: A new pilot program is developing regenerative farming research for tea and other perennial crops, addressing a research gap. This initiative may establish frameworks and evidence needed for sustainable transition in tea supply chains.

title: Forest-protection law gets EU Commission green light, but leather loophole is unacceptable – Greenpeace
title_en: EU Anti-Deforestation Law Gets Green Light; End-2026 Compliance Deadline
summary_en: The European Commission has approved the EU anti-deforestation regulation (EUDR) with full application required by end-2026. This direct regulatory deadline affects coffee and tea sourcing practices and compliance obligations for JDE across all supply chains.

title: Kenya Remains Committed to Tea Reforms
title_en: Kenya Remains Committed to Tea Reforms
summary_en: The Kenyan government proposes new legislation to allow tea producers to bypass the traditional Mombasa tea auction and sell directly. This reform could reshape tea supply chain dynamics and farmer economics for JDE's tea sourcing regions.

title: New AI Irrigation Management System for Tea Gardens
title_en: New AI Irrigation Management System for Tea Gardens
summary_en: Taiwan's Ministry of Agriculture (TBRS) has announced development of an AI irrigation system for tea cultivation. This technology advancement could improve productivity and sustainability in tea supply regions.

title: Study Reveals Coffee’s True Impact on Deforestation
title_en: Study Reveals Coffee's True Impact on Deforestation
summary_en: A new study reassesses coffee's deforestation footprint relative to beef, palm oil, and soybeans. Findings directly inform EU regulatory frameworks (EUDR) on deforestation-risk commodities and affect JDE's coffee sourcing compliance obligations.

title: Is Brazil’s Specialty Coffee Sector on the Rise?
title_en: Is Brazil's Specialty Coffee Sector on the Rise?
summary_en: After below-average production, Brazil is forecast to deliver a bumper coffee harvest. Supply recovery from the world's largest coffee producer will impact availability and pricing for European importers.
